title: Used GPU sold as working, but artifacts under load
question: Should the seller (payee) keep the escrowed funds, given the card was advertised as fully working but artifacts under load and fails the agreed benchmark threshold?

evidence.label: Original listing text describing the card as fully working with no artifacting
Buyer's 3DMark Time Spy result run after delivery
Buyer's screen capture and short clip showing green and pink artifacts during a stress loop
Seller's pre-shipment benchmark and photo of the card on a test bench

terms: Seller agreed to ship a Radeon RX 6800 that is fully functional with no visual artifacts under sustained gaming or stress load. The parties set a pass threshold of a 3DMark Time Spy graphics score of at least 9,500, to be confirmed by the buyer within 72 hours of delivery on a clean driver install. If the card artifacts under load or scores below 9,500 on that test, the buyer may return it and the escrow refunds to the buyer; otherwise the funds release to the seller.
